methods.description: This method describes the setup of preindustrial and mid-Pliocene simulations following CMIP5 and PlioMIP protocols, respectively. It outlines the differences in boundary conditions, greenhouse gas concentrations, and the integration process for climate modeling.

steps.description: The preindustrial simulation follows the standard experimental protocols of CMIP5, which also serves as the control simulation for PMIP3.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up preindustrial simulation using CMIP5 protocols

steps.description: The solar constant is 1365 W m −2 and the concentrations of greenhouse gases are set to 280 ppmv for CO2, 760 ppbv for CH4 and 270 ppbv for N2O.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure solar constant and greenhouse gas concentrations for preindustrial simulation

steps.description: For the mid-Pliocene experiment, we adapted the protocols of Experiment 2 in PlioMIP, which is designed for AOGCM simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: Adapt PlioMIP Experiment 2 protocols for mid-Pliocene simulation

steps.description: The topography and land surface conditions (e.g., vegetation types and ice sheet) are changed according to the PRISM-3D dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: Update topography and land surface conditions using PRISM-3D dataset
outputs: Updated topography and land surface conditions

steps.description: The ocean model was started by adding the temperature anomalies between PRISM-3D dataset and LEVITUS data to the initial field of the ocean temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: Initialize ocean model with temperature anomalies from PRISM-3D and LEVITUS data

steps.description: The mid-Pliocene simulation has been integrated for 1000 yr.
The last 100 yr outputs of the mid-Pliocene simulation are used in this study for the climatology and compared to the PI experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze last 100 years of mid-Pliocene simulation for climatology comparison

methods.description: This method provides a detailed description of the FGOALS-g2 model components and their configurations, which are used for climate simulations in this study.

steps.description: The coupled AOGCM used in this study is the FGOALS-g2 developed at LASG, IAP, CAS.
steps.searchable_summary: Use FGOALS-g2 AOGCM for climate simulations

steps.description: The model includes four components: GAMIL2.0, LICOM2.0, CICE4-LASG, and CLM3.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure FGOALS-g2 with GAMIL2.0, LICOM2.0, CICE4-LASG, and CLM3 components

steps.description: The GAMIL2.0 employs a hybrid horizontal grid with Gaussian grid and 26 vertical layers.
The ocean model LICOM2.0 has a horizontal resolution of 1°×1° and 30 layers in vertical.
The two-step shape-preserving advection scheme (TSPAS) has been introduced.
These four components are coupled by the NCAR coupler version 6 (CPL6).
